design_philosophy.core_loop: Select event → execute button-mashing or skill-based input → achieve time/score → compete across multiple Olympic disciplines for medal standings
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Execution precision vs. computer AI competency that scales with difficulty level; luck factor in certain events
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Compete as a national Olympic athlete across authentic Barcelona 1992 events with realistic country-specific strengths
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces authentic Olympic scoring (medal-based points system, position-dependent ranking) and deliberately varies input methods by event (button-mashing for sprints, deliberate input for diving/pole vault, slow-paced for swimming). Refuses to simplify diving into predefined sequences—player must control jumps, risking 0.0 scores but enabling skill expression beyond 9.0 marks.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three explicit difficulty levels (club, national, Olympic) that fundamentally alter computer athlete performance—Olympic level features record-breaking AI and heavy randomness; game noted as easy for experienced players except sprinting, hurdles, and hammer throw events which rely on execution and chance

progression.unlock_mechanism: Practice mode available; mini-Olympics (with selectable events) or full Olympic competition; progression through event sequence within competition structure
replayability_hooks: Eight playable nations each with unique anthem and country-specific athlete strengths (Germans strong in archery, Italians in swimming, Russians in pole vault, Americans in sprinting)
Computer athletes have fictional names with individual stat profiles and specializations
Skill ceiling in diving and pole vault—experienced players can exceed expected scores (9.0+ on 3.5-difficulty dives, 6.35m pole vault clears)
Randomness factor in sprinting/hurdles/hammer at higher difficulties creates variance across runs
Mini-Olympics mode allows custom event selection

world_building_techniques: Licensed Barcelona 1992 Olympics setting with authentic event roster
Country-specific anthem snippets and national identity for eight playable teams
Coca-Cola branding integration (European version)—zeppelin sponsor placement above scoreboard with jingle
Fictional but nationality-contextualized computer athletes with believable strengths/weaknesses profiles

faction_indicators: Playable nations: France, Great Britain, Germany, Italy, Japan, Spain, Unified Team (Soviet Union), United States
Country-specific athlete archetypes and specialization patterns (German archery dominance, Italian swimming excellence, Russian pole vault strength, American sprint prowess)
danger_signals: Olympic difficulty level features computer athletes breaking world and Olympic records with regularity
Heavy reliance on randomness at highest difficulty introduces luck-based failure states
environmental_storytelling: Barcelona 1992 Olympics setting with authentic venue context
Eight authentic Olympic events: 100m, 110m hurdles, 200m freestyle, hammer throw, archery, pole vault, 3m springboard diving
Medal scoring system follows official Olympic point distribution (gold=24, descending to 13 for 12th place)
